_note: Long-tail pharmacology. Specific dosing, interactions, contraindications nurses must know.

beta_blockers.mechanism: Block beta-adrenergic receptors; reduce HR, contractility, BP

metoprolol.dose: 25-200mg PO BID or 5mg IV q5min x3 for ACS

nursing_considerations: Monitor for bronchospasm in COPD patients
Never abruptly discontinue (rebound tachycardia, HTN crisis)

ace_inhibitors.mechanism: Block angiotensin-converting enzyme; reduce BP, afterload; prevent cardiac remodeling

adverse_effects.dry_cough: 10-15% of patients; bradykinin accumulation; switch to ARB if intolerable
adverse_effects.hyperkalemia: Monitor K+; especially with CKD or K-sparing diuretics
adverse_effects.angioedema: Rare but life-threatening; African Americans higher risk; contraindicated if prior angioedema
adverse_effects.hypotension: First-dose effect; start low in elderly or volume-depleted

ace_inhibitors.black_box_warning: TERATOGENIC — Category D in pregnancy; causes fetal renal failure, oligohydramnios, death

warfarin.mechanism: Vitamin K antagonist; inhibits factors II, VII, IX, X
warfarin.monitoring: INR; goal 2-3 for DVT/PE/AFib, 2.5-3.5 for mechanical valve
warfarin.reversal: Vitamin K 2.5-10mg PO/IV (onset 6-24 hours); FFP or PCC (immediate)

drug_interactions.effect: Decreases INR; may need double warfarin dose
GI bleed risk; use with caution
Kill gut flora making vitamin K; INR may rise

warfarin.food_interactions: Vitamin K-rich foods (kale, spinach, broccoli) lower INR; consistency matters more than avoidance

apixaban.dose: 5mg PO BID (2.5mg if ≥2: age >80, weight <60kg, Cr >1.5)

doacs.advantage_over_warfarin: No INR monitoring, fewer drug interactions, predictable dosing
doacs.disadvantage: Expensive, some irreversible (except dabigatran), contraindicated if CrCl <30

spectrum.ampicillin: Broader; covers Listeria, Enterococcus; first-line for GBS in pregnancy
spectrum.amoxicillin: Better PO absorption; add clavulanate for beta-lactamase producers

penicillins.allergy_crossreactivity: Cephalosporin cross-reactivity <2% if no anaphylaxis history; avoid if prior anaphylaxis

vancomycin.use: MRSA, C. diff (PO form), coag-negative staph
vancomycin.dosing: 15-20 mg/kg IV q8-12h; goal trough 15-20 mcg/mL for serious infections
vancomycin.monitoring: Trough before 4th dose; also SCr and hearing (ototoxicity)
vancomycin.red_man_syndrome: Histamine release; flushing, hypotension; slow infusion to 2 hours, premedicate with diphenhydramine
vancomycin.nephrotoxicity_risk: Increases with aminoglycosides, NSAIDs, loop diuretics

common_concentration_errors: Using patient weight in pounds instead of kg (2.2x error)
